author_style: Riccardo Carlesso has a conversational and informal writing style. He often uses humor and personal anecdotes to illustrate his points. He is also a big fan of Google Cloud Platform and often writes about its various services.

articles_feedback.title: What is toilet paper’s right side?
articles_feedback.summary: The author discusses the different ways to hang toilet paper and provides a detailed explanation of the pros and cons of each method. They also share their own personal preference for hanging toilet paper.

articles_feedback.title: Spaghetti Bolognese don’t exist!!1!
articles_feedback.summary: The author argues that spaghetti Bolognese is not a real dish and provides several reasons to support their claim. They also discuss the history of spaghetti Bolognese and how it became so popular.

articles_feedback.title: Migrate GCP projects across organizations, the gcloud way
articles_feedback.summary: The author provides a detailed guide on how to migrate GCP projects across organizations using the gcloud command-line tool. They also discuss some of the challenges they faced during the migration process and how they overcame them.

articles_feedback.title: My favourite Toolchain
articles_feedback.summary: The author shares their favorite toolchain for software development. They discuss the pros and cons of each tool and explain why they prefer it over other options.
articles_feedback.song: I've Got a Tool for That

articles_feedback.title: Ruby on Rails-like ORM and scaffolding in Golang anyone?
articles_feedback.summary: The author explores the possibility of creating a Ruby on Rails-like ORM and scaffolding in Golang. They discuss the challenges they faced and the potential benefits of such a project.

articles_feedback.title: My Mac’s battery on Google Cloud Monitoring — with SMS if its low
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to inject a generic key/value into Google Cloud Monitoring and set up alerts on it. They use this to monitor their Mac's battery level and receive SMS alerts if the battery is low.

articles_feedback.title: Canary Deployment on GCP with Cloud Deploy
articles_feedback.summary: The author demonstrates how to perform canary deployments on GCP using Cloud Build and Cloud Deploy. They discuss the benefits of canary deployments and how to implement them using Google Cloud's CI/CD tooling.

articles_feedback.title: Setting a ★ Cloud Build trigger with Pulumi in  Python
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to set up a Cloud Build trigger using Pulumi in Python. They discuss the benefits of using Pulumi and how to integrate it with Cloud Build.

articles_feedback.title: Google Forms to Datastudio
articles_feedback.summary: The author shows how to use Google Forms, Google Spreadsheets, AppsScript, and Datastudio to compare aggregated data into nice graphs, massaging data through some “unpivoting” first.
